findings.finding: Phase 3 already_indexing response may return a job_id for a scan started before the current commit, causing the worker to declare correlation success without the new doc indexed

findings.evidence: Plan: 'On already_indexing response, wait on the returned in-flight job_id'; node_semantic.py uses _indexing_lock to serialize scans, so an in-flight scan that pre-dates the commit cannot be re-triggered for the post-commit state

findings.finding: Phase 3 per-job tracking dict is process-local and lost on uvicorn restart between POST and polling GET
findings.claim: If the Jimmy semantic service restarts between a worker's POST to /api/research/reindex and its status GET to /api/research/reindex/<job_id>, the client receives a 404 with no recovery path defined.
findings.evidence: Phase 3 deliverable: 'tracks per-job state in a process-local dict (rows added, completion time, error)'; no persistence layer or restart-safe fallback is described anywhere in the plan

findings.finding: Phase 4 assumes /retrieve hits include an `intended_path` field and a numeric score, but the retrieve endpoint response schema is unverified in any cited excerpt
findings.claim: If the /retrieve endpoint returns hits under different field names or omits a path-matchable field, the post-commit verification logic will silently misclassify every doc as unverified.
findings.evidence: Phase 4 deliverable: 'Match returned hits by intended_path; require non-noise score (threshold ≥0.4)'; api/routes/retrieve.py is in the file tree but its response schema is absent from all cited excerpts

findings.finding: Phase 4 adds status: indexing_pending to CompletedRecord with no backward-compatibility handling for existing completed.jsonl records
findings.claim: Any code that branches on status ok|error will mishandle records written before Phase 4 ships if the deserialization path does not supply a default for missing or unknown status values.
findings.evidence: Phase 4 deliverable: 'Add status: indexing_pending to CompletedRecord schema (third state, distinct from ok/error)'; no migration step or default-value handling for pre-existing completed.jsonl entries is mentioned

findings.finding: Phase 4 re-enqueue of indexing_pending records creates duplicate completed.jsonl entries for the same document with no deduplication
findings.claim: When a re-queued record is processed successfully, _append_completed is called a second time, producing two completed entries for the same document and corrupting queue history.
findings.evidence: Phase 4 deliverable: 'Worker re-enqueues indexing_pending records once for verification retry'; excerpt line 857: '_append_completed' appends unconditionally; no tombstoning or idempotency key is described

findings.finding: Phase 5 per-host Ollama serializing lock specifies no locking primitive and no stale-lock cleanup on process crash

+
+async function enqueueEnrichContentSectionsIfNeeded(row: CliQueueRow, output: string): Promise<void> {
+  if (row.command !== '/enrich-content' || !isAwaitingSections(output)) {
+    return;
+  }
+
+  const { count, error: existingError } = await getSupabaseClient()
+    .from('cli_queue')
+    .select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })
+    .eq('project_id', row.project_id)
+    .eq('command', '/enrich-content-sections')
+    .in('status', ['queued', 'running']);
+
+  if (existingError) {
+    throw new Error(
+      `Failed to check existing /enrich-content-sections rows: ${existingError.message}`
+    );
+  }
+
+  if ((count ?? 0) > 0) {
+    return;
+  }
+
+  const ownerId = typeof row.params.owner_id === 'string' ? row.params.owner_id : undefined;
+  const { error: insertError } = await getSupabaseClient().from('cli_queue').insert({
+    project_id: row.project_id,
+    command: '/enrich-content-sections',
+    params: normalizeQueueParams(row.project_id, {
+      project_id: row.project_id,
+      ...(ownerId ? { owner_id: ownerId } : {}),
+    }),
+    status: 'queued' as const,
+  });
+
+  if (insertError) {
+    throw new Error(`Failed to enqueue /enrich-content-sections: ${insertError.message}`);
+  }
+
+  console.log(`[chain] enqueued /enrich-content-sections for project=${row.project_id}`);
+}

+function decodeJwtPayload(token: string): Record<string, unknown> | null {
+  const segments = token.split('.');
+  if (segments.length < 2) return null;
+  try {
+    const normalized = segments[1].replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/');
+    const padded = normalized.padEnd(Math.ceil(normalized.length / 4) * 4, '=');
+    return JSON.parse(atob(padded)) as Record<string, unknown>;
+  } catch {
+    return null;
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Ensure the authenticated caller owns the requested website project.
+ *
+ * Service-role JWTs bypass this check. Regular callers must provide a bearer
+ * token whose `sub` matches `website_projects.owner_id`.
+ */
+export async function assertCallerOwnsProject(
+  req: Request,
+  projectId: string
+): Promise<Response | null> {
+  const authHeader = req.headers.get('Authorization');
+  const bearerToken = authHeader?.replace(/^Bearer\s+/i, '').trim() ?? '';
+
+  if (!bearerToken) {
+    return jsonResponse({ error: 'Authorization header required' }, 401);
+  }
+
+  const payload = decodeJwtPayload(bearerToken);
+  if (!payload) {
+    return jsonResponse({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, 401);
+  }
+
+  if (payload.role === 'service_role') {
+    return null;
+  }
+
+  const callerId = typeof payload.sub === 'string' ? payload.sub : null;
+  if (!callerId) {
+    return jsonResponse({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, 401);
+  }
+
+  const supabaseUrl = Deno.env.get('SUPABASE_URL');
+  const serviceRoleKey =
+    Deno.env.get('SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY') ?? Deno.env.get('VITE_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY');
+
+  if (!supabaseUrl || !serviceRoleKey) {
+    return jsonResponse({ error: 'SUPABASE_URL or SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY not configured' }, 500);
+  }
+
+  const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, serviceRoleKey);
+  const { data: project, error } = await supabase
+    .from('website_projects')
+    .select('owner_id')
+    .eq('id', projectId)
+    .maybeSingle();
+
+  if (error) {
+    return jsonResponse({ error: 'Failed to verify project ownership', detail: error.message }, 500);
+  }
+
+  const ownerId = (project as { owner_id?: string | null } | null)?.owner_id ?? null;
+  if (!project) {
+    return jsonResponse({ error: 'Project not found' }, 404);
+  }
+
+  if (ownerId !== callerId) {
+    return jsonResponse({ error: 'Forbidden' }, 403);
+  }
+
+  return null;
+}
+

+/**
+ * Write user-submitted competitors from the WorkfloDock spine into Synapse.
+ *
+ * Two targets:
+ *   - `competitors` table (canonical): INSERT one row per competitor with
+ *      { brand_id, name, website, social_handles: { weakness } }. Idempotent
+ *      via name match — existing rows for this brand_id + name are skipped.
+ *   - `brand_uvps.competitors` text[]: UPDATE latest UVP row to mirror names.
+ *     Only updated if a uvp row already exists (no INSERT here).
+ *
+ * Returns a count so the caller can log it on workflodock_submissions.result.
+ * Never throws — all DB failures are swallowed + logged.
+ */
+async function writeCompetitorsToSynapse(
+  competitors: { name: string; url?: string; weakness?: string }[],
+  brandId: string,
+  synapseUrl: string,
+  synapseKey: string
+): Promise<{
+  inserted: number;
+  skipped_existing: number;
+  skipped_invalid: number;
+  insert_failures: number;
+  uvp_mirrored: boolean;
+}> {
+  if (!Array.isArray(competitors) || competitors.length === 0) {
+    return {
+      inserted: 0,
+      skipped_existing: 0,
+      skipped_invalid: 0,
+      insert_failures: 0,
+      uvp_mirrored: false,
+    };
+  }
+
+  const normalizeCompetitorName = (name: string): string => name.trim().normalize('NFC').toLowerCase();
+
+  const headers = {
+    Authorization: `Bearer ${synapseKey}`,
+    apikey: synapseKey,
+    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
+    Accept: 'application/json',
+  };
+
+  // Look up existing competitor names for this brand to avoid duplicates.
+  const existingNames = new Set<string>();
+  try {
+    const resp = await fetch(
+      `${synapseUrl}/rest/v1/competitors?brand_id=eq.${brandId}&select=name`,
+      { headers }
+    );
+    if (resp.ok) {
+      const rows: { name?: string }[] = await resp.json();
+      for (const r of rows) {
+        if (typeof r.name === 'string') existingNames.add(normalizeCompetitorName(r.name));
+      }
+    }
+  } catch (err) {
+    devLog(
+      'competitors existing-names lookup error:',
+      err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
+    );
+  }
+
+  const dedupedCompetitors = new Map<
+    string,
+    { name: string; url?: string; weakness?: string }
+  >();
+  let skippedInvalid = 0;
+  for (const competitor of competitors) {
+    if (typeof competitor?.name !== 'string') {
+      skippedInvalid += 1;
+      continue;
+    }
+
+    const trimmedName = competitor.name.trim().normalize('NFC');
+    if (trimmedName.length === 0) {
+      skippedInvalid += 1;
+      continue;
+    }
+
+    const normalizedName = normalizeCompetitorName(competitor.name);
+    if (!dedupedCompetitors.has(normalizedName)) {
+      dedupedCompetitors.set(normalizedName, {
+        ...competitor,
+        name: trimmedName,
+      });
+    }
+  }
+
+  const dedupedRows = Array.from(dedupedCompetitors.entries());
+  const skippedExisting = dedupedRows.filter(([normalizedName]) =>
+    existingNames.has(normalizedName)
+  ).length;
+
+  const toInsert = dedupedRows
+    .filter(([normalizedName]) => !existingNames.has(normalizedName))
+    .map(([, competitor]) => ({
+      brand_id: brandId,
+      name: competitor.name,
+      website: competitor.url ?? null,
+      social_handles: competitor.weakness ? { weakness: competitor.weakness } : {},
+    }));
+
+  let inserted = 0;
+  let insertFailures = 0;
+  if (toInsert.length > 0) {
+    try {
+      const resp = await fetch(`${synapseUrl}/rest/v1/competitors`, {
+        method: 'POST',
+        headers: { ...headers, Prefer: 'return=representation' },
+        body: JSON.stringify(toInsert),
+      });
+      if (resp.ok) {
+        const rows = (await resp.json()) as unknown[];
+        inserted = Array.isArray(rows) ? rows.length : toInsert.length;
+        devLog(`wrote ${inserted} competitor rows to Synapse for brand ${brandId}`);
+      } else {
+        insertFailures = toInsert.length;
+        const text = await resp.text();
+        devLog('competitors insert failed:', resp.status, text.slice(0, 200));
+      }
+    } catch (err) {
+      insertFailures = toInsert.length;
+      devLog('competitors insert error:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
+    }
+  }
+
+  // Mirror names into latest brand_uvps.competitors text[] (UPDATE only).
+  let uvpMirrored = false;
+  try {
+    const uvpResp = await fetch(
+      `${synapseUrl}/rest/v1/brand_uvps?brand_id=eq.${brandId}&select=id&order=created_at.desc&limit=1`,
+      { headers }
+    );
+    if (uvpResp.ok) {
+      const uvpRows: { id: string }[] = await uvpResp.json();
+      if (uvpRows.length === 1) {
+        const names = competitors
+          .map((c) => c?.name?.trim())
+          .filter((n): n is string => !!n && n.length > 0);
+        if (names.length > 0) {
+          const patchResp = await fetch(`${synapseUrl}/rest/v1/brand_uvps?id=eq.${uvpRows[0].id}`, {
+            method: 'PATCH',
+            headers,
+            body: JSON.stringify({ competitors: names }),
+          });
+          uvpMirrored = patchResp.ok;
+          if (!patchResp.ok) {
+            const text = await patchResp.text();
+            devLog('brand_uvps.competitors patch failed:', patchResp.status, text.slice(0, 200));
+          }
+        }
+      }
+    }
+  } catch (err) {
+    devLog(
+      'brand_uvps.competitors mirror error:',
+      err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
+    );
+  }
+
+  return {
+    inserted,
+    skipped_existing: skippedExisting,
+    skipped_invalid: skippedInvalid,
+    insert_failures: insertFailures,
+    uvp_mirrored: uvpMirrored,
+  };
+}
